content:
Incident Report
Page 7 of 11
I had Kimmel remove his primary handgun from his duty belt, which I then photographed and conducted a round count, with Detective Sergeant Engelbertson verifying the count. His handgun was a Glock 22 .40 caliber (SN: PLS524) . I was able to verify one live .40 caliber round in the chamber and (15) additional rounds in the magazine. Kimmel produced two additional magazines, which each of them had 15 rounds. Kimmel also produced a backup handgun from his duty belt, which was a .40 caliber Glock 23 (SN: UPT525) . I was able to see a live round in the chamber and (13) additional rounds in the magazine. Kimmel also removed an AR magazine from his plate carrier. I had counted (30) rounds in the magazine. I took photographs of the equipment before reloading the magazines and I provided them back to Kimmel.
Neither Clarkson or Kimmel were willing to provide a statement at that time. Kimmel cited his reason as being too tired.
Shortly after, I departed Lacey PD and responded to the incident location, where I arrived at approximately 0000 hours on 9/4/20. The scene at the apartment complex was marked with crime scene tape around the perimeter. Upon our arrival, I observed a large number of individuals dressed in all black attire with black bandanas covering their faces. These individuals began yelling obscenities towards investigators that were on scene.
Upon entering the scene, I observed a deceased male subject, identified as Reinoehl, laying on the ground, near what was identified as a vehicle Reinoehl was last seen inside. The suspect's vehicle also had what appeared to be bullet holes through the windshield. There were also multiple unmarked vehicles surrounding Reinoehl's vehicle. I also observed numerous yellow placards marking spent shell casings.
While we were on scene, Detective Sergeant Engelbertson, Detective Robb, and I were tasked with inventorying the vehicles that were still on scene.
The first vehicle we were assigned to inventory was a Pierce County marked K9 patrol vehicle (WALIC: XMT C8752C) assigned to Pierce County Deputy John Munson. Detective Sergeant Engelbertson was assigned as a scribe to write down any items of interest. I was tasked with taking photographs of the vehicle and of any items of interest as Detective Robb would remove items of interest, such as various types of ammunition. This also included a Daniel Defense AR rifle that was found on the driver side floorboard, leaned up against the driver seat. I also assisted Detective Robb in conducting round counts of the ammunition, which we then relayed to Detective Sergeant Engelbertson what the round counts were.
We also inventoried another vehicle, which was a dark blue unmarked Dodge Charger (WALIC: AXR0804) who we were told was driven by one of the US Marshall's. Detective Sergeant Engelbertson, Detective Robb and I carried out the same assignments.
For a detailed description of round counts as well as what was inventoried, see Detective Sergeant Engelbertson's report.
After conducting an inventory of the second vehicle, we were advised we could clear the scene. I cleared the scene shortly after.
rplwdir.x1
09/14/20
